versions.author: JayOnBlue
versions.description:
1. UI/UX fidelity pass (matched against your 15 screenshots): colored letter-icon squares on area cards + "N PAGES" uppercase footer, role-picker icons ($, ⚙, {}, ⓘ), tech-nav icons + folder icons in the source tree, flowing "Term — definition" glossary on a tinted background, a PLACEHOLDER callout style for stub pages, per-contributor avatar colors (hashed per name, not per-release), coverage bars that always render (grey/empty when n/a), an AI Review block restructured to match (status word + generic note + plain "Security:" bullets), a rebuilt Impact panel with the exact "blast radius" copy, a 2-column Depends-on/Used-by layout, "Related Pages" cards on component/object detail, Methods with a humanized one-line description + explicit "▸ signature & used-in" toggle, color-coded coverage %, "+ Auto-generated" pill, "Purpose (interpretive — editable)" label, and — the biggest one — search rebuilt as a real centered command-palette modal instead of an inline dropdown, since that's what your screenshot showed.

2. Version History upgrade: each commit now shows its full description (the commit body, not just the subject line) plus two derived summaries — Technical (component counts by type: added/modified/removed) and Business (which Feature, from the same dependency-graph clusters the Features page uses, those components belong to). Both are honestly labeled as deterministic cross-references, not AI prose.

3. Workflow prompt optimized for scale: the old version inlined the full git diff truncated at 12,000 characters — on a huge repo that silently drops changes past that point. Rewrote author-business-docs.mjs to never inline diffs at all: Claude gets a manifest and reads current file content itself, changes are grouped by feature (bounded, focused calls instead of one mega-prompt), and any feature with too many changed files gets chunked into batches — so a million-line change becomes many small accurate calls instead of one that quietly loses detail.

Verified with real jsdom tests (20 checks: all 14 routes + search modal + role picker + filters + AI review/impact/methods + version history + changelog + download modal) — all passing — plus the version-history and author-business-docs scripts tested directly against a real git repo with multi-line commit messages.
